title: container: do not create .containerenv with -v SRC:/run
body:
if /run is on a volume do not create the file /run/.containerenv as it
would leak outside of the container.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14577

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

<!--

something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
If /run is on a volume then the file `/run/.containerenv` is not created automatically anymore
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

`/run/secrets` is coming from `mounts.conf`.  Should we add a similar check there?  Somehow it is an explicit configuration and the user can override it.

For `--init`, I'd suggest to use the root directory instead of `/run`:

```
	ContainerInitPath = "/podman-init"
```

I've added a patch to this PR
I am not crazy about creating these files like podman-init in / since this forces us to create an inode that ends up in the image.  Of course /run/.containerenv is not much better.
We could default the init to `/run/podman-init` and fall back to `/podman-init` if `/run` is a bind-mount to the host.

let's skip this patch for now.   It is a corner case (it needs `--init` and `/run` bind mounted) and it is not clear what path would work in all cases.  `.containerenv` instead is a clearer fix because it is always created
